clues.clue: This calm fourth-grader with a football-shaped head lived in a big city boarding house.
In a 1991 Klasky-Csupo series, this screwdriver-wielding baby in a blue shirt led the diaper crew.
This journal-keeping cartoon kid crushed on Patti Mayonnaise and had an alter ego, Quailman.
Two dam-building brothers named Norbert and Daggett were this buck-toothed rodent.
This 1998 Nicktoon starred conjoined feline-and-canine title characters sharing one body.
Three sugar-and-spice sisters battled the simian villain Mojo Jojo.
This lab-coated boy genius hid a secret laboratory from his sister Dee Dee.
This buff, pompadour-sporting ladies' man in shades constantly struck out with women.
Ed, Edd n Eddy ran scams to buy these spherical, jaw-straining candies.
This cowardly pink dog protected elderly owners Muriel and Eustace from the paranormal.
Scrooge McDuck's grandnephews are Huey, Dewey and this third triplet.
Chip 'n Dale headed a squad of crime-solving rodents in this 1989 detective series.
In TaleSpin, this easygoing bear pilot flew the cargo plane known as the Sea Duck.
Darkwing Duck's heroic catchphrase ended with the line 'Let's get...' this adjective.
In Goof Troop, this clumsy Disney everyman raised his son Max.
The zany Warner siblings were Yakko, Wakko and this little sister.
These two lab mice, one a genius and one insane, schemed nightly to take over the world.
clues.response: Who are Pinky and the Brain?

clues.clue: This 1992 animated series starred the Dark Knight of Gotham and his butler Alfred.
This clawed, gruff X-Men hero has a healing factor and an adamantium skeleton.
Tiny Toon Adventures and Animaniacs were both produced by this blockbuster filmmaker, Steven.
These half-shell heroes were 'teenage,' 'mutant' and 'ninja.'
The 'One Saturday Morning' block aired on this alphabet broadcast network.
This bumbling cyborg detective relied on niece Penny and a dog named Brain to crack cases.
Captain Planet's powers came from rings of Earth, Fire, Wind, Water and this fifth element.
This 1990 eco-hero was summoned when five Planeteers combined their rings.

final.clue: This stuttering Looney Tunes character closed many shows with 'Th-th-th-that's all, folks!'
